name: Custom Rogers substrate with wideband loss model
description: Tests that the agent creates a custom dielectric for a material not in the catalog, applies a DjordjevicSarkar dispersion model, and verifies properties with getMaterialProperties
prompt: I need to model a Rogers 4350B substrate for a 5 GHz bandpass filter design. Set up the substrate with accurate wideband loss modeling and show me how permittivity and loss tangent vary from 1 to 10 GHz.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates RO4350B as a custom dielectric with explicit EpsilonR and LossTangent, since it is not in the catalog
assertions.expected: dielectric(Name=... EpsilonR=3.66 or similar, LossTangent=0.0037 or similar)

assertions.description: Uses DjordjevicSarkar frequency model for wideband accuracy
Uses getMaterialProperties to compute frequency-dependent values
Does not attempt to load RO4350B from the catalog, which would error

name: 5-layer stripline stackup with realistic conductors
description: Tests that the agent builds a multi-layer pcbComponent with correct property ordering (BoardThickness before Layers) and assigns a real metal instead of PEC
prompt: Build a 5-layer PCB stackup for a stripline: top ground, 0.8mm FR4, signal trace (20mm x 2mm), 0.8mm FR4, bottom ground. The grounds are 20mm x 15mm rectangles. Use copper conductors. Show the result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets BoardThickness before Layers to avoid the dielectric thickness overwrite warning
Assigns a real metal conductor instead of relying on PEC default
Uses 5 elements in Layers: {metal, dielectric, metal, dielectric, metal}
assertions.expected: Layers cell array with 5 elements alternating trace/dielectric

name: Browse catalog and select low-loss substrate
description: Tests that the agent uses DielectricCatalog to explore available materials rather than guessing or hardcoding
prompt: I'm designing a low-loss antenna feed network at 10 GHz. What substrate materials are available in MATLAB's built-in catalog with loss tangent below 0.003? Show me the catalog and help me pick the best one.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses DielectricCatalog to browse available materials
Accesses the Materials property to inspect the catalog table
Filters or inspects LossTangent values from the catalog rather than guessing material properties
assertions.expected: Reference to Loss_Tangent column or LossTangent values from the catalog table

name: Upgrade substrate to frequency-dependent after design()
description: Tests that the agent can switch a designed component's substrate to DjordjevicSarkar post-design without recreating it
prompt: I used design() to create a microstrip line at 2.4 GHz with FR4. Now I want to upgrade the substrate to use a frequency-dependent loss model for more accurate wideband results. How do I do that without redesigning from scratch?

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets FrequencyModel on the existing substrate
Sets Frequency on the existing substrate for the reference point
Modifies the existing object's Substrate property in-place, does not call design() again
